As they all made their way to Paul’s home, he sent voice notes saying he was going to “chop this kid up now” in earshot of his mum and sister.

Mr Little said Maxwell had recruited her 6ft1ins former kickboxing champion son after the issue arose between her and Paul.

He added: “The reason they are all there is because of her.



“She not only encouraged it, she championed it. She was the director and producer of that violent movie.”

Mr Little said despite this, all three had a role to play in the fatal stabbing.

The court heard Clegg knew where Paul lived and directed her family to his home that night while carrying the knives in her bag.

She also lured the victim to the door and despite urging “don’t actually stab him”, she was part of the team and knew there was an intention to kill Paul, it was said.

Clegg later lied about her and her brother’s names and told the ambulance service she didn’t know the address she was at or how Paul suffered his injuries.



She was jailed for a minimum of 19 years and 96 days, while her mum received a minimum term of 22 years and 96 days.

Liam Donlin was jailed for at least 25 years and 98 days.

Detective Superintendent John McNamara of Lancashire Police said: “From spending time with Paul’s family throughout the course of the investigation, and reading their impact statements, it quickly became clear to myself and the team how special and loved Paul was.

“Nothing will bring him back to them, his killers may have been given a life sentence, but so have Paul’s loved ones, and our thoughts are very much with them at this time.

“That night Paul opened his front door to his then 21-year-old cousin in the early hours of the morning. He didn’t expect that to be the last thing he did.



“This goes much further back than 22nd June 2025. Joanne Maxwell let her upset that Paul didn’t defend her fester for almost a year before she incited her son to kill him. Liam Donlin may have been the one to stab Paul, but there were three people responsible for his murder.

“Amie Clegg lied to the ambulance service, wasting time where they could have been making attempts to save Paul trying to deceive those dedicated to helping him.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“Liam callously stabbed Paul as soon as he opened the door that night. He went to Paul’s house with the intention of taking his life.

“Paul died in a brutal, unexpected attack in the place he should have felt the safest, and in the company of those he should have been safe with – in his home, with his family.”

News.title: Heartstopping moment passenger plane narrowly avoids catastrophic crash with cargo jet while landing at airport
News.content:
THIS is the spine chilling moment a passenger plane and a cargo jet narrowly avoid a catastrophic smash in a radar blunder.

The two planes were both flying in the airspace at Newark Liberty International Airport in New Jersey at the time of the close call.





A video of the flight map shows the moment the two aircrafts were just seconds away from tragedy, directly heading towards a collision.

The planes on the map show the Fedex cargo jet (labelled FDX721) curve around.

Its path was taking the plane directly into the route of the Alaska Airlines flight (labelled ASA294).

The video from FlightRadar24 shows just how close to disaster the flights were on Tuesday evening.

The Alaska Airlines flight had been ordered to perform a-go-around when the FedEx flight was cleared to approach the intersecting runways for landing, according to the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA).

The passenger plane avoided the chartered cargo jet just 300 feet.

Air traffic controllers directed the Alaska flight to reroute just seconds before it was due to land, according to audio of the moments ahead of the crossover.



Former vice president of the FAA Michael McCormick told ABC 7 New York that the terrifying ordeal had happened because of the two intersecting runways.

“It is a challenge for a tower controller to try to get that timing perfect, it doesn’t always work and that’s what happened in this case,” he said.

“So the tower controller waited and unfortunately, in my opinion, too long and they had to send the aircraft on a go-around.”

An investigation has been launched by the FAA and the National Transportation Safety Board into the near crash.

The continuing partial government shutdowns in the United States have resulted in significant staffing shortages in major airports across the country.



TSA workers are currently not being paid for the hours they are working.

To date, White House economists have estimated the cost of the shutdowns to be upwards of $2.5 billion in losses.

Last week, Senate Democrats blocked a bill for the fourth time this month that would have resparked funding for the Department of Homeland Security.

Delta Air Lines CEO Ed Bastian has slammed Congress for the continued shutdown.

He called politicians’ refusal to settle the funding fight “inexcusable”



RECOMMENDED STORIES

“We’re outraged,” Bastian said.

The partial shutdown entered its 34th day on Friday.

News.title: UK border chief in charge of stopping small boats QUITS after mounting pressure over failure to reduce Channel crossings
News.content:
BORDER FAILURE UK border chief in charge of stopping small boats QUITS after mounting pressure over failure to reduce Channel crossings

THE UK’s border chief who was tasked with stopping the small boats crisis has quit after failing to bring the numbers down, The Sun can reveal.

Martin Hewitt, the border security commander, will be out of the role by the end of the month.





An insider told The Sun that the change comes amid a drive for a “tighter” operational focus.

More than 40,000 people came across the Channel in dinghies last year, with numbers set to soar in the coming weeks as warmer weather arrives.

Hewitt took on the top job in October 2024 with 30 years’ experience across policing and crisis management.

He had led the National Police Chiefs’ Council for four years to 2023 and previously served as an assistant commissioner in the Metropolitan Police.



Sources told The Sun that Hewitt made the decision to step down and had been “frustrated” about the role.

Duncan Capps, former Commandant of the Royal Military Academy Sandhurst, is understood to be stepping into the role on an interim basis.

He is currently serving in another senior job within the Border Security Command.

An insider told The Sun: “There was a view that the objectives were not being met, and there needed to be a tighter, more defined operational focus.”



Last year Hewitt told The Sun he wanted to be judged on his ability to bring Channel crossings down after taking up the new role.

During a visit to Tunisia when he was three months into the job he said: “Definitely judge us on the impact we’ve had on the number of crossings.

“Because that is absolutely clear to me from the Prime Minister, from the Home Secretary and I’m sure from the Foreign Secretary.

“That’s what my job is here to try and deliver. So that’s absolutely, that is the measure.”

In October Hewitt told the home affairs select committee that it was “frustrating” that French authorities were not stopping more boats.



Asked about when a new tactic would come into force which would allow French cops to intercept boats at sea, he said: “I was over, and met with him in Paris three weeks ago to really press the point about how significant delivering this maritime tactic is for us because of the taxi boat scenario.

“This was referred to by President Macron at the summit in July when he spoke with the [British] prime minister.

“So it is frustrating that it’s taken the time that it has.

“Your point about the political instability, clearly that has been a backdrop.”

A Home Office spokesperson said: “We would like to thank Martin Hewitt CBE QPM for his dedicated leadership since the creation of the Border Security Command.



“Over the past 18 months, the Border Security Command has brought government agencies, law enforcement and international partners together to tackle people smuggling gangs, as well as seeing the Border Security, Asylum and Immigration Act into law.”

The shake up comes as Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood attempts to get a grip on the near-constant arrivals of illegal migrants across the Channel.

She faces having to water down her tough immigration crackdown amid a potential Labour civil war over her hardline approach.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Doubts have emerged over whether Labour will fully apply stricter settlement rules to migrants already living in Britain.

The backlash intensified after ex-Deputy PM Angela Rayner branded the plans “un-British”, fuelling expectations of a behind-the-scenes compromise.

News.title: Ex-Lidl manager with ‘fear of rejection sparked by ADHD’ wins £45k payout after suing for ‘unfair dismissal’
News.content:
FORK SAKE Ex-Lidl manager with ‘fear of rejection sparked by ADHD’ wins £45k payout after suing for ‘unfair dismissal’

A FORMER Lidl manager has won a huge £45,000 payout after successfully suing the supermarket for unfair dismissal.

Ryan Toghill was fired for gross misconduct after he was caught driving a forklift against the orders of his boss.





But a tribunal hearing into his sacking ruled bosses at Lidl failed to properly account for his ADHD and its impact on his behaviour.

He was accused of flouting instructions by hopping on a forklift despite his boss telling him not to.

But Ryan argued that he struggled with manual work due to a hernia.



Ryan said it was “commonplace” for staff to use the heavy-duty machines without a proper sign-off.

He joined Lidl in Newport, South Wales, in 2019 and climbed the ranks to deputy manager by June 2022.

Just weeks earlier, he had been diagnosed with ADHD. A condition the tribunal heard left him acutely sensitive to criticism and rejection, and plagued by anxiety.

After moving to a store in Ystrad Mynach in 2023, new boss Ms Ogden made it clear he must not use the machinery.



But colleagues later reported he had been secretly using it, and he was even waiting for certain staff to leave before doing so.

A disciplinary hearing found him guilty of ignoring orders, withholding information and “deliberate deception” and he was sacked.

Bosses also claimed he showed no remorse.

But the tribunal found that was down to his condition, not his attitude.

Employment Judge Samantha Moore ruled Lidl failed to properly account for how ADHD affected his behaviour.



She said his condition caused “intense sensitivity to the impression of being rejected, harassed, or criticised” and meant he could appear disengaged or indifferent.

The judge added he suffered “ADHD paralysis”, struggled to process questions and needed extra time to respond, putting him at a “substantial disadvantage” during disciplinary proceedings.

While Lidl was entitled to treat misuse of the equipment as gross misconduct, the tribunal found the dismissal itself was unfair.

Ryan was awarded £45,147 – including £20,000 for injury to feelings.

Speaking previously to The Daily Mail, Ryan said: “Regarding the judgment, it’s of course great to receive some kind of justice for the treatment I was subjected to.



“It’s a little frustrating and disappointing how backlogged the system is, as this took around 18 months from start to finish, which really shows how frequently things like this are happening.

“I would say I’m ‘content’ with the end result, not happy. The sum awarded doesn’t cover the amount of money I lost – and continue to lose – as unfortunately, I’m still out of work.

“My health has dramatically declined since the incident and I still find myself unable to seek employment.”

He added: “Due to having ADHD and little else to do, I hyper-focused the hell out of my case despite having a solicitor, as it’s the only thing I could focus on at all.

“The result of that, is my knowledge of employment law is pretty great, and I’ve actually been trying to help others in similar situations. I’ve had a few people reach out to me on Reddit asking for my advice.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

“In a perfect world, I might look into making a career out of it, but at the moment my health has to come first as it’s very much holding me back.”

The Sun has approached Lidl for comment.

News.title: Banksy’s surprising net worth revealed after street artist’s identity ‘unmasked by old police report’
News.content:
STREET artist Banksy is sitting on a staggering £20million fortune – as claims emerge he’s finally been unmasked.

The elusive graffiti genius has piled up a jaw-dropping £19.1million fortune, with another £1million tucked away in property investments.





Company accounts reveal the artist, long shrouded in secrecy, even paid £174,000 in Corporation Tax in 2024.

The firm, NTS Services Limited, was incorporated in March 2020 to provide “management services”.



During its first few months of operation it “maintained client relationships” that led to “high profitability”.

The business was set under what has been reported as his current legal name, David Jones.

The company’s original name was not NTS Services but, Nothing To See Limited.

A nod to the phrase “nothing to see here”, famously used in his 2015 Dismaland project.



The business is reportedly registered under the name David Jones – now believed to be the artist’s latest alias.

That name has been linked to another firm connected to Banksy’s long-time associate, accountant Simon Durban, fuelling claims the mystery man has been hiding in plain sight.

Investigators believe “David Jones” is actually Robin Gunningham.

He is a Bristol-born man who has been rumoured for years to be the person behind the spray cans.

A probe by Reuters claims Gunningham changed his name to the ultra-common “David Jones” to dodge detection after previously being outed.



It comes after The Sun revealed earlier this week that police records from a boozy night out in New York listed the artist as Robin Gunningham.

Reuters also claims passport details match between Gunningham and Jones and that he even travelled under the new name to Ukraine, where Banksy created a series of wartime murals.

He reportedly left the country on the same day as Massive Attack star Robert Del Naja, who has also been tipped in the past as the man behind the art.

Banksy first burst onto the scene in Bristol in the late 1990s, becoming the world’s most famous graffiti artist while fiercely guarding his identity.

It’s long been claimed that Gunningham, a former pupil at Bristol Cathedral School, first adopted the name “Robin Banks” before evolving it into the now-iconic Banksy.



But despite decades of speculation, and now a multi-million-pound empire laid bare, the artist’s true identity still remains one of the art world’s biggest open secrets.

News.title: Our quiet village is overrun by Iran war plane spotters weeing & clogging up roads – they’re treating it like a holiday
News.content:
VILLAGERS near an RAF base say they have been swamped with Iran war plane spotters who are reportedly causing traffic chaos and weeing everywhere.

The airbase, which is currently hosting US bombers amid the war in the Middle East, has been a focal point for national and foreign plane spotters.





Visitors camp outside the RAF base for days in their cars and caravans – using ladders to see over the screens that were installed to stop them from viewing the site.

But the constant influx of plane spotters is upsetting locals, who say they cause antisocial behaviour, “clog up the roads” and use fields surrounding the base as a “toilet”.

Locals say that hundreds of plane spotters travel through the village of Marston Meysey, on the border of Wiltshire and Gloucestershire, every day – as it is within walking distance of RAF Fairford.

The area has become increasingly popular with the spotters since USAF bombers began arriving at the base earlier this month.

Villager Dave James, 71, retired, said he sees hundreds of spotters everyday.

He said he would like to see all the spotters “away from the village” and see more police patrolling.

He added: “We need something in place, to deter them to stay away from here.



“Why should we be overrun with them?

“These spotters, are they in favour of fuel and oil prices going up then? Which is what this is causing.

“There’s foreign cars, up the lane, one is from Netherlands so he’s travelled all the way from Holland.

“They are treating it like a holiday.



“They are obviously in support of what’s happening in Iran.

“If Donald Trump has started it that’s why we’ve got this out here and so they must be supporting whatever Donald Trump is doing.

“I’d like to get all them spotters away from here – out of our community.”



Mr James said they are “clogging up the roads”.

He added: “The road is not very wide up through this village and we got people that don’t have off street parking.

“If they are clogging our village up with their cars, people come home from work and they can’t park outside their own property.”

He said that some plane spotters sit in fields around the airbase and “don’t care” about who they belong to.

Him and his partner Chrissie James have allotments along the public footpath to RAF Fairford and have had to put cones up to stop the public from parking there.



Mr James added: “They don’t care whose properties it is.

“The farmers are trying to grow grass for signage and they are trampolining it all day.

“It’s a public footpath – they are up there with their chairs, they’re all sat down .

“They screened off the fencing, but they got ladders so they’re looking over the airfield still – so who’s policing these signs?

“We seem to be a forgotten community out here.



“We shouldn’t have to put up with the hassle of all those spotters I think.”

Mrs James, 77, also retired, added: “Some of the plane spotters get quite abusive and they block people’s entrances.

“It wears you down a bit.

“It’s a war for God’s sake, it’s not a pleasure.

“And they are leaving a mess as well.”



Kirsty Payne, 57, a charity worker, said they generally have plane spotters coming in regularly, but ever since RAF Fairford has been used by the US they have been “absolutely swamped” with them.

She said they have come along and “parked everywhere”, making it hard for ambulances and the school bus to get through and cause antisocial behaviour.

Ms Payne added: “They have no facilities down there.

“We can’t walk the dogs down there anymore because there’s toilet paper and human excrement in the bushes.

“They are weeing everywhere – they’re there for hours and hours.



“There’s people climbing up trees, everyone’s on their stepladders.

“Wildlife and hedgerows will suffer from people urinating and defecating.

“They also leave rubbish behind which isn’t great for the wildlife either.”

She added that not all plane spotters are to blame, but that a small minority is “making it much worse” for the residents.

A plane spotter who didn’t want to be named that said: “I’m here for the day, I’m at my car and I’ve got sandwiches.



“I love aircrafts so that’s why I come here.”

Cllr Martin Smith, Cabinet Member for Highways, said: “We are aware that the arrival of additional US bombers at RAF Fairford has led to a sharp increase in visitor numbers, with many plane spotters travelling into nearby villages, including Marston Meysey.

“We recognise the concerns raised by residents about congestion, inconsiderate parking, and the potential impact on road safety.

“Responsibility for ensuring highway safety, maintaining emergency access, and responding to any immediate obstructions lies with the police.

“Over the weekend, officers issued warnings where appropriate, and our Civil Enforcement Officers were present to issue Penalty Charge Notices (PCNs) to vehicles parked in contravention of existing restrictions, such as on double yellow lines. We will continue to monitor these areas as part of our routine enforcement work.



“Where no formal parking restrictions are in place, the council is not able to take enforcement action.

“However, we remain willing to work with the local community and parish councils to explore future options for managing parking pressures, should they wish to pursue new measures.

“Residents who encounter vehicles causing an active obstruction or posing a safety risk should report these directly to the police, who have the necessary powers to respond.”

Cllr Joe Harris, cabinet member for highways at Gloucestershire County Council, said: “We have put temporary traffic management measures in place on roads around RAF Fairford, in response to requests by the police and RAF Fairford to address safety and access issues.

“A number of traffic cones have been installed along the verge and the shared pedestrian/cycleway on Whelford Road after vehicles were parking on the route, causing obstructions and raising safety and security concerns.



“In addition, a road closure has been introduced on Dunfield Road, so local residents can still access their properties at all times. All of the measures currently in place have been agreed following discussions with Gloucestershire Constabulary and RAF Fairford.

“The situation will continue to be kept under review, with the safety of residents, pedestrians, cyclists and road users remaining the priority.”

Wiltshire Police Inspector Scott Anger said: “We have been working closely with colleagues from Gloucestershire Constabulary and the MOD, as well as engaging with the Marston Meysey Parish Council to address these concerns.

“We have been carrying out regular patrols to support the local community.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Insp Anger added: “I was present in Marston Meysey today (19/03) and engaged with some of the aircraft enthusiasts there today and I saw that everyone was parked in a considerate manner and were well behaved. We appreciate that the warm weather has attracted more people and we are asking people to be mindful if they visit the village.



“It is worth bearing in mind that screens have now been put in place by the RAF, so the view is limited.”

News.title: Netflix boss quietly unfollows Meghan Markle amid claims of ‘difficult’ relationship
News.content:
Netflix boss quietly unfollows Meghan Markle amid claims of ‘difficult’ relationship

The streaming giant’s CEO Ted Sarandos has unfollowed both the Duchess of Sussex and her lifestyle brand As Ever, as rumours swirl that their partnership with Netflix is in jeopardy

View 4 Images Meghan Markle returned to Instagram last year to promote her lifestyle brand As Ever (Image: Getty Images for Fortune Media)

Netflix CEO Ted Sarandos has quietly unfollowed both Meghan Markle and her lifestyle brand As Ever’s Instagram accounts, amid claims that the relationship between the streaming giant and the Sussexes is souring.

‌



The Netflix boss was one of the first big names in the entertainment industry to follow Meghan when the Duchess of Sussex returned to Instagram in 2025. In March last year, Meghan launched an account for her l ifestyle business , first as American Riviera Orchard before it was rebranded to As Ever, which Mr Sarandos added to his carefully curated list of around 450 accounts he follows. However, last month both Sarandos and Netflix CCO Bela Bajaria unfollowed both accounts.

‌



Kate's secret message to Camilla as she and William stray from tradition READ MORE :

View 4 Images Netflix CEO Ted Sarandos has unfollowed the Duchess of Sussex on Instagram (Image: John Angelillo/UPI/Shutterstock)

‌



Mr Sarandos's wife, Nicole Avant, does still follow Meghan on Instagram - but not As Ever, as a Netflix source told the Daily Mail : “Nicole and Meghan are still friends.” The source did not elaborate on Meghan’s relationship with Sarandos. The Mirror has contacted Harry and Meghan's representatives and Netflix for comment.

The quiet unfollowing comes after Variety published a scathing exposé into Harry and Meghan’s relationship with the streamer, with several inside sources claiming their partnership is in jeopardy. The insiders claim that their deal had suffered, as claims were made that the Sussexes were difficult to deal with and were ‘poor communicators’.

Sources also claimed that Netflix bosses were ‘blindsided’ by both the 2021 Oprah Winfrey interview and Harry’s 2023 memoir Spare. One such claim from an insider states that during virtual and in-person meetings with Netflix, Meghan tends to talk over or recast Harry’s thoughts, sometimes while he is mid-sentence, usually preceded by a touch to the arm or thigh.

‌



View 4 Images A recent report claims Harry and Meghan's relationship with Netflix is in jeopardy (Image: Getty)

Meghan’s lawyer Michael J. Kump hit back at the claims, telling Variety that this assertion “seems calculated to play into the misogynistic characterization of her bossing her husband around.” Prince Harry, meanwhile, attests that this is “categorically false”.

Within hours of the tell-all article being published, Netflix chief content officer Bela Bajaria publicly denied the claims of a dispute, briefly addressing the speculation at the Next on Netflix event on Wednesday. Bajaria reiterated that Netflix still has a partnership with the couple, saying: "We still have a relationship with them. We have movies in development with them. They have things in development on the TV and film side.”

‌



She added that Netflix “had an amazing documentary with them,” referring to their six-part docuseries titled Harry & Meghan, which was released in December 2022, and documented the early days of the relationship and their decision to step back from their royal roles.

View 4 Images The Sussex's first project with the streamer was their 2021 tell-all docuseries (Image: Netflix)

‌



Bajaria added of their deal: "Deals come and go all the time, and we don’t renew so many deals, those just don’t get as much press for obvious reasons. There’s no juicy story there." Despite Bajaria’s claims, another Netflix insider told the Daily Mail’s Alison Boshoff that staff at the streamer would not air their grievances with the couple in public.

The source said: “No one is willing to be other than supportive in public” as they added that there is potential for a new season of With Love, Meghan, however: “I don’t think anyone is going to be killing themselves with an effort to work with them at this point.”

Meghan and Harry signed a "multi-year, first look deal for film and television projects" with Netflix in 2025, which was considered a downgrade from their previous deal, signed in 2020, which was reported to be more than $100 million (£74 million). The Mirror has contacted Harry and Meghan’s representatives, and Netflix for comment.

News.title: Oncologist says 'vital to see GP' if you have symptoms of 'silent killer' cancer
News.content:
Oncologist says 'vital to see GP' if you have symptoms of 'silent killer' cancer

There are key red flag signs to look for and a full list of potential symptoms

View 3 Images Jaundice can be a symptom

People are being urged to watch out for the signs of a 'silent killer' cancer that is said to be one of the hardest to spot. Diagnosis often comes late for the illness because of the hidden signs .

‌



Researchers say there are two key symptoms of pancreatic cancer to be aware of that could help doctors diagnose it early. This includes yellowing of the skin (jaundice) and bleeding in the stomach or intestine. Two other lesser-known and unrecognised warning signs of pancreatic cancer could also be increased thirst and dark yellow urine, according to researchers.

‌



“When pancreatic cancer is diagnosed earlier, patients have a higher chance of survival,” said Dr Weiqi Liao, data scientist at the University of Oxford. “It is possible to diagnose patients when they visit their GP, but both patients and GPs need to be aware of the symptoms associated with pancreatic cancer.”

‌



View 3 Images The pancreas is a vital internal organ

‌



“Being aware of the early symptoms of pancreatic cancer is crucial if we are to diagnose patients earlier and improve pancreatic cancer survival,” added Dr Pippa Corrie, chair of the pancreatic workstream of the NCRI Upper Gastrointestinal Group and Consultant Medical Oncologist at Addenbrooke’s Hospital in Cambridge, UK.

“This research could help GPs and their patients know more about the signs of pancreatic cancer. It’s vital that people speak to their GP if they notice these symptoms.”

There is a long list of other symptoms that were also found in a recent study:

Article continues below

Problems swallowing

Diarrhoea

Change in bowel habits

Vomiting

Indigestion

Abdominal mass

Abdominal pain

Weight loss

Constipation

Fat in stool

Abdominal swelling

Nausea

Flatulence

Heartburn

Fever

Tiredness

Appetite loss

Itching

Back pain

View 3 Images You could have pain and swelling

Pancreatic cancer is more likely for those aged over 75 and is not common for people under 40, according to the NHS. People can reduce their chances of getting the cancer by cutting down on red and processed meat, not being overweight, not smoking and drinking less alcohol.

News.title: Met Police officer raped and sexually assaulted child after chatting online
News.content:
A volunteer Metropolitan Police officer, who now identifies as a woman, raped and sexually assaulted a child.

James Bubb, who now identifies as a woman named Gwyn Samuels, groomed one of two victims online before sexually assaulting her when she was just 12 years old.

Samuels was convicted of raping and sexually assaulting the girl following a trial last summer. The 28-year-old was also convicted of raping a woman whom the defendant met online whilst pretending to be a 16-year-old girl.

The former special constable received a total sentence of 32 years, comprising 24 years' imprisonment and a further eight years on extended licence, at Aylesbury Crown Court on Friday.

Sentencing the defendant, Judge Jonathan Cooper said: "You abused the most intimate trust of each of your victims. In each case you systematically groomed your victim, first to befriend them, then to abuse them, finally to bend them to your will.

"This was a campaign of abuse against each that was intended to break their will entirely."

Image: PA) PA)

Throughout the trial the defendant was referred to by their biological sex, but during the first sentencing hearing last week, the judge said that since they have transitioned, they should be known by their new identity of Gwyn Samuels. Jurors at the trial heard the officer sexually assaulted the girl in public shortly before her 13th birthday. The trial heard Samuels was violent towards the girl when the defendant raped and sexually abused her in her early teens – with the victim telling police the 28 year old choked and punched her.

Amersham Law Courts was told Samuels met their first victim on chat roulette site Omegle in 2018 before meeting face-to-face for the first time at a Christian festival several months later. The girl said the defendant appeared "paranoid" when they were with her and she was being "hidden" when they were in public together – telling police her festival colour-coded child wristband was clearly visible.

'Significant risk to women'

Samuels's first victim also said the defendant spoke "a lot about the powers he had" in their role with the Met as a special constable. The court was told the officer raped their second victim, a woman who had just turned 18 when she met the defendant, whilst they were in an on-off relationship between January 2018 and February 2023.

In relation to the first victim, Samuels was convicted of one count of raping a child under 13, one count of sexual activity with a child, one count of assault of a child under 13 by penetration, and one count of assault by penetration. The defendant, of Chesham, Buckinghamshire, was cleared of one count of rape and one count of sexual activity with a child in relation to that complainant, and convicted of one count of rape against the second victim.

All charges took place between January 1, 2018 and April 2, 2024. The judge stated that he understands the defendant, who has been held in a male prison whilst in custody, will continue to be detained there.

"The key point is that you are a very significant risk to women," Judge Cooper declared. "This is not predicated on your trans status, but on your criminal status."

Image: PA) PA)

Samuels was also made subject to a lifelong sexual harm prevention order, prohibiting them from contacting young women.

Addressing Samuels' victims personally after the defendant had left the courtroom, the judge expressed: "Over time, I hope the fact that you are survivors of abuse is ultimately more important to your future lives than the fact you were victims of it. I have to acknowledge that your ordeal at the hands of this defendant was made infinitely more challenging by your experiences of the criminal justice system.

"There were delays and frustrations at every turn. At various times you felt that power was in the hands of the defendant. Ultimately, power was in your own hands, and particularly in your own voices. The power of your evidence was heard and recognised by the jury in this case."

Paul Robson, a solicitor from the Crown Prosecution Service, commented: "The defendant targeted these victims and exploited their vulnerability and trust. When the victims asked for the abuse to stop, they were ignored.

"The defendant had clearly sought to attain positions of trust within the community to gain the trust of the victims and the people around them. The offending has had a profound effect on the victims, and we are very grateful to them for their unwavering support throughout this prosecution.

"As this case demonstrates, we will prosecute those who exploit and harm others, and hope this serves as a warning that justice will catch up with those who offend in this manner."

Investigating officer Detective Sergeant Kirsty Pendle stated: "I want to pay tribute to the incredible courage and strength shown by both victims in coming forward and supporting this investigation through to court proceedings. Their bravery has been instrumental in bringing this offender to justice.

"We keep an open mind as to whether Gwyn Samuels, formerly known as James Bubb, has committed any other offences. If you have any information about this defendant, please contact us quoting Operation Aspect. You can visit a police station, call 101, or leave details via our website."

News.title: Kanye West's £42million home turned into an end of the world concrete bunker
News.content:
Kanye West's £42million home turned into an end of the world concrete bunker

Kanye West bought a stunning Malibu beach house for $57m in 2021 and stripped it down to concrete and steel in a bunker project that lost him millions

View 4 Images Kanye West had huge plans for the property (Image: Pascal Le Segretain, Getty Images)

Built in 2013, this stunning Malibu beach house was something of a dream until Kanye West bought it and appeared to have considerably different ideas – or so he believed.

‌



Covering 4,000 square feet, with four bedrooms and five bathrooms, this substantial property sits directly on the sands, raised above the coastline. Its peaceful location provided the most calming ocean views, situated amongst affluent and famous neighbours.

‌



The artist purchased the property in 2021 for an eye-watering $57million and commenced a transformation nobody was quite ready for. In fact, his proposals seemed so absurd to the project manager it led to a lawsuit against West.

‌



Tony Saxon was the former project manager and property caretaker for West, who disclosed the star wanted to transform the home into a "bomb shelter from the 1910's".

View 4 Images Kanye West lost $29 million on the home (Image: BACKGRID)

With this in mind, they were tasked with demolishing marble bathrooms and stripping out windows, plumbing and electricity, and he even wanted to remove the stairs and replace them with slides – which he received a firm no to.

‌



According to the new buyer, who picked up the property for $21 million after it was on the market for $54 million, the home is constructed entirely from concrete and steel.

Content cannot be displayed without consent

In a TikTok video offering an empty house tour, he said: "If there was a fire or tsunami, this house would still be erected; it wouldn't be destroyed like all the other houses." He added: "Apparently he just wanted to have an open flame for a heat source and just protection from the elements."

‌



However, according to the former project manager, his rationale for isolation was much more profound. Saxon suggested that the rapper didn't want to be a "slave" to modern life and sought to evade being "accessible" to the government.

View 4 Images The rapper wanted to add slides instead of stairs (Image: The Oppenheim Group/TNI PRESS LTD.)

He detailed West's plans, telling NBC: "He only wanted plants. He only wanted candles. He only wanted battery lights. And he just wanted to have everything open and dark ...

‌



"You can't keep food in that house, because you had no refrigerator left ... You had no windows. I had seagulls flying in."

Odds of NASA astronauts returning from Moon, AI turning us all into a brainless army, the North Sea asteroid, and why you should be grateful to have an appendix - all this and more in our latest weird science newsletter

The eccentric plans didn't end there. The music tycoon harboured aspirations for a private Wi-Fi network and an alternative energy source. However, the real problem arose when he expressed a desire for no doors, windows or fixtures, just vast expanses of concrete.

‌



In the house tour video, guests are shown an underground tunnel beneath the building, with a ladder from the main house leading down into it.

View 4 Images The home was bought for $21 million - yet there's no doors or windows (Image: BACKGRID)

They also unveiled markings scratched into the concrete walls in West's handwriting. There are several doodles and messages inscribed with stone, and some on stones, left behind.

Article continues below

Regrettably, the neighbours have been left with quite an eyesore, as they look out from their multi-million pound homes onto this dilapidated building.

One neighbour, according to the new owner, constructs shopping centres in the Midwest, whilst the other property belongs to an Israeli diamond merchant.

In the process of gutting this property right down to nothing but concrete and metal, West lost $29 million dollars. The new owner hopes to invest some money back into renovating it to be the perfect Malibu beach house it once was and sell it for its original price of $57 million.

News.title: 'Give our illegal home to housing charity - don't just demolish it'
News.content:
'Give our illegal home to housing charity - don't just demolish it'

Rose Murray pleaded with a court for her illegally built home to be given to a charity while blasting 'absolutely barbaric' of her family following a 20-year court battle

View Image Chris and Rose Murray built their expansive home at Faughan Hill without permission (Image: Barry Cronin)

A couple who built their 6,220 square-foot home have pleaded with authorities to donate the property to a housing charity instead of demolishing it as a local council starts tearing it down.

‌



Chris and Rose Murray illegally built their expansive home at Faughan Hill, in Bohermeen, County Meath, Ireland in 2006 despite having been refused planning permissiong from Meath County Council. The pair built a home that was reportedly twice the size of the originally proposed property despite the denial, and it has since been the subject of multiple planning refusals and appeals.

‌



That lengthy legal saga came to a close on Monday, when the council took possession of the family home, with the demolition commencing in earnest on Thursday despite protests from its owners - who are pursuing a human rights case.

‌



HMRC allowance for workers abolished as April 2026 UK tax changes confirmed READ MORE :

UK retailer closing all stores in April after collapsing into administration READ MORE :

Speaking on Monday, Mrs Murray said in an emotional statement that she and her family were being "made homeless" while pleading for their home to be given to charity. She said the family, including the couple's 19 and 24-year-old children, "could have been left on the street yesterday with the shirts on our back".

She said they were forced out of the home on Monday, when her son was told to pack a few items and leave, and her daughter was refused entry to the property after returning from the gym to retrieve her clothes.

The mum, who said she recently recovered from breast cancer, said she wasn't allowed to retrieve her prescription, adding: "It's absolutely barbaric." She and her husband have accepted that what they did was wrong, stating: "Everybody, everybody has regrets, but we held our hands up 20 years ago and say yes, what we did was wrong."

‌



But she has aggressively opposed the bid to demolish her home, stating while the order was still pending that it should be saved and repurposed.

She said: "The bit that gets me is this demolition order is pending at the momen. Why not give it to charity? I would love to see it go to a reputable charity." A High Court judge ruled on Thursday that the demolition orders remain enforcable, despite the Murray's legal representatives asking for the process to be pauded pending an application to the European Court of Human Rights.

The couple have alleged violations of their human rights, but the demolition could be seen going ahead on Friday morning as heavy machinery arrived at the home, reducing the large building to rubble.

‌



The demolition was conducted by a security team of 13 vehicles, with Meath County Council insisting in a statement that it had engaged with family and community representatives on site while being assisted by gardaí. They facilitated the removal of personal belongings and household items in line with a decision made by the court.

A spokesperson for the council said: "The Court has directed that the Council, with the assistance of An Garda Síochána, take possession of the property and proceed with demolition in accordance with existing court orders.

"The Council, with the assistance of An Garda Síochána, engaged on site with family and community representatives to facilitate the removal of personal belongings and household items, in line with the Court’s direction. The Council acknowledges the cooperation of those involved in this process.

Article continues below

"As this is an ongoing legal matter, Meath County Council is unable to comment further at this time."

News.title: 85p spice you should add to your dinner plate to help with digestion
News.content:
85p spice you should add to your dinner plate to help with digestion

It may well already in your cupboard, but this cheap spice could do more than add flavour - experts say it may quietly support your digestion

View 2 Images Better gut health doesn't have to break the bank (Image: Copyright 2022 Monica Bertolazzi)

A common kitchen staple costing as little as 85p could be the secret to better digestion. Experts say adding it to your meals may offer more than just flavour.

‌



Andy Briggs, supplement expert at XO Fitness, told The Mirror that cinnamon is a simple and affordable way to support gut health. He labelled it “the 85p spice you should add to your dinner plate to help with digestion.”

‌



Cinnamon has been used for centuries in traditional medicine. According to Healthline, it contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help support overall digestive health.

‌



One of its key benefits is its ability to help regulate blood sugar levels. Research cited by the National Center for Biotechnology Information in America suggests cinnamon may slow the breakdown of carbohydrates in the digestive tract, which can help stabilise blood sugar and ease digestive strain.

Cinnamon may also help reduce bloating and discomfort. According to Medical News Today, its natural compounds can support digestion by reducing gas and promoting smoother gut function.

The spice also contains antimicrobial properties. Research reported by PubMed indicates cinnamon may help fight harmful bacteria in the gut, potentially improving the balance of your microbiome.

‌



View 2 Images Cinnamon (Image: Getty Images)

Digestive health is closely linked to gut bacteria balance. As stated by the NHS, maintaining a healthy gut can support immunity, nutrient absorption and overall wellbeing.

While cinnamon can be beneficial, it works best as part of a balanced diet. Experts say adding small amounts to meals such as curries, stews or even desserts can be an easy way to incorporate it daily.

‌



Briggs also highlights other low-cost ways to support gut health. He says electrolytes, which can cost as little as 43p per sachet, help maintain hydration and support smooth digestion.

He adds that oats, bananas and live natural yoghurt can also play a role. These foods provide fibre, prebiotics and probiotics, which according to experts help feed beneficial gut bacteria and keep digestion running smoothly.

Oats, which can cost around 85p, are rich in beta-glucan fibre, while bananas starting from 16p contain prebiotics and potassium. Live yoghurt, often available for under £1, contains beneficial bacteria that support the gut lining and improve digestion.

Article continues below

Experts say small, consistent dietary changes can make a big difference over time. Adding affordable ingredients like cinnamon could be a simple step towards better digestive health.

News.title: Ricky Hatton's dating history - from Claire Sweeney to 'brutal' engagement
News.content:
Ricky Hatton's dating history - from Claire Sweeney to 'brutal' engagement

Boxing legend Ricky Hatton died aged 46 leaving behind a colourful romantic history including TV star Claire Sweeney and a turbulent 11-year engagement with Jennifer Dooley

View 6 Images Boxing legend Ricky Hatton with his former partner Claire Sweeney (Image: @claire.sweeney/instagram)

Boxing legend Ricky Hatton was "in the best place he had been in for years" before his shock death, his family said today.

‌



The boxing icon was discovered at his £1.7 million residence in Hyde, Greater Manchester on September 14 last year, and the inquest into his death is taking place today.

‌



His manager Paul Speak, who found Hatton, has attended, alongside Jennifer Dooley - Hatton's ex-partner, Campbell, the boxer's son, his dad Ray, and his brother Matthew, and other family members.

‌



At the inquest, Campbell told the court his father was "very happy, always very laid back, except when it came to sport". He said: "He lived his life in and out of the ring at a hundred per cent and as he always said he never regretted it."

He added that his father was "fearless" and was "always generous with people around him" but had struggled with his mental health.

Ricky Hatton inquest UPDATES: Mum details last hug as manager describes finding body READ MORE :

"We felt he had been in the best place he had been in for years," Campbell added.

‌



The legendary fighter, nicknamed The Hitman, crossed paths with former girlfriend Claire Sweeney, 54, on ITV's Dancing On Ice in January 2024 and the couple quickly became inseparable, with the duo photographed arm-in-arm enjoying a spirited pub crawl across Greater Manchester.

Claire, who had been contentedly single prior to encountering the Stockport-born trainer, found herself captivated by his charisma, sparking an "electric chemistry", a source revealed to the Mirror. Sadly, their relationship came to an end in December 2024. Reports suggested their romance ran its course, though they stayed on amicable terms.

Yet Claire wasn't the sole woman to fall for the boxer's charm – here we examine the late champion's love life.

‌



Claire Sweeney

Ricky, a former light welterweight champion, encountered Claire whilst rehearsing for the ITV reality programme and set about courting her. A source disclosed to the Mirror: "The chemistry was electric. Ricky went out of the show early on but he contacted her afterwards and let's say he started wooing her. They've been out on several dates."

View 6 Images Claire Sweeney and Ricky Hatton (Image: Getty Images)

The source continued: "They've been seeing each other quite a bit. He is completely besotted." The source revealed: "It seems like an unlikely match but he's always had a way with women. They fall for his personality, and Claire has. He knows her number one priority is her son so they are being very careful. Let's watch this space."

‌



Liverpool-born Claire previously had a relationship with Daniel Reilly, with whom she shares 11-year-old son Jaxon, which ended in 2017.

In a candid interview on the Mirror's Invite Only podcast, she explained her priorities: "I really want to be with someone who makes me laugh. Someone who's kind. Someone I will click with. So, someone like that, it just wouldn't be about aesthetics – OK, there's an initial attraction but I want it to be more than aesthetics, that's the type of person I'm looking for."

Unfortunately, the romance between Ricky and Claire proved short-lived, with reports emerging in December 2024 that they'd parted ways, though they continue to be amicable.

‌



Chelsea Claire

Shortly before taking to the ice on Dancing on Ice, Ricky split from Playboy model Chelsea Claire, who was 14 years younger than him. Their brief relationship kicked off in summer 2023, though they kept things private until going public in October that year.

View 6 Images Ricky Hatton's ex-girlfriend, Playboy model Chelsea Claire was 14 years younger than him

Whilst the relationship appeared promising initially, professional obligations ultimately took their toll on the couple. A source revealed to The Sun: "Ricky and Chelsea had a great time together, but it ended up being more of a fling. They are at very different stages of their life and Ricky is really throwing himself into training and wants to concentrate on that. Things are still really amicable and they will be friends going forward."

‌



Angela Blemmings

Beauty specialist Angela Blemmings started dating Ricky in July 2022, with the pair confirming their relationship after posting a photograph from their romantic Dublin break.

Angela joined Ricky at the BBC Sports Personality Of The Year awards in December, though their relationship came to an end in 2023.

Marie Pollard

Ricky and Marie Pollard dated in 2016, though their relationship was short-lived as they separated the following year. Yet five years later, they reignited their romance and announced to fans that they were an item once more.

‌



6 View 6 Images Marie Pollard and Ricky Hatton (Image: Twitter) 6 View 6 Images The couple reunited some years later (Image: WireImage)

The couple posted pictures from their romantic Croatian break in 2021. An insider at the time said: "He's known Marie for years, but they've grown close again. Ricky's really happy."

‌



Jennifer Dooley

Ricky was already dad to son Campbell Hatton, a boxer, with ex-partner Claire when he started seeing Jennifer Dooley in 2005.

A statement was read out on her behalf at the inquest today, where she described how she and Ricky met in 2005 and "shared many happy experiences" thanks to his "unique career as a professional boxer". In 2011, they welcomed their first daughter, followed by their second daughter in 2017, she told the court. The family enjoyed "special" trips to Tenerife, she added.

Hatton attended sports days and was "always proud' of their daughters" achievements, the inquest heard. He would see the girls several afternoons each and often take them out for dinner. They would often "be silly together and do TikTok dances," Dooley said. She added that Hatton could however be "forgetful".

‌



By September 2025, he was making "positive plans for the girls" and looking forward to going to Dubai, added Ms Dooley. The girls were excited to see him box, she said. She added they have been left "heartbroken" by his death, which left a "significant hole in their lives". His daughters have lost a "fun-loving daddy," she adds.

The couple, who share daughters Millie and Fearne, got engaged in 2009 after Ricky proposed on Valentine's Day. However, he ominously dubbed their Cheshire abode the 'Heartbreak Hotel', in homage to Elvis Presley's iconic song. In 2010, a video surfaced of Ricky using cocaine, prompting him to seek rehab treatment whilst Jennifer stood by his side.

View 6 Images (Image: Getty)

‌



Yet, just a month later, Irish boxing champion Emma Bowe disclosed to the Mirror that she had been having an 11-month affair with the boxer after meeting him for an autograph in Dublin.

The couple split in 2013, with Ricky leaving their £3million family home, but they reunited a year later.

Ricky remarked at the time: "Me and Jennifer are working through our difficulties. We are no different to anybody else except my life is under the spotlight because of who I am."

‌



Things seemed to be looking up when the family took a holiday in Tenerife over the New Year period.

However, in 2016, following another string of alcohol-fuelled nights out for Ricky, his 11-year relationship with Jennifer ended after he was snapped looking dishevelled whilst partying with other women.

Speaking to the Daily Star about his ex-fiancée, he admitted: "It's very upsetting when you've been in such a long-term relationship and it comes to an end. I can't deny there are times I have found things difficult."

Article continues below

News.title: Melania Trump's 'concerns' for Barron as US conscription fears grow
News.content:
Melania Trump's 'concerns' for Barron as US conscription fears grow

As the war between the US and Iran intensifies, speculation grows that America could introduce a military draft, raising questions about whether 20-year-old Barron Trump could be called up to fight

View 3 Images Melania Trump wrote about her concerns for her son Barron (Image: Getty)

As the conflict between America and Iran intensifies, fears are mounting over the possibility of the USA deploying ground troops into the country.

‌



Underlying these worries is the prospect that the Donald Trump administration might commence drafting its citizens in readiness for a terrestrial battle.

‌



Should such a draft be implemented, it could result in some of America's young men and women being conscripted to serve their nation. This is an eventuality that has not occurred since the early 1970s when 1.8 million were summoned to fight in Vietnam, a duty Trump famously evaded five times due to a common medical condition.

‌



Consequently, there is conjecture that Donald Trump's youngest son, 20-year-old Barron, could be called upon to fight; a scenario that may worry his mother Melania.

Melania has previously expressed her concerns about her only child for entirely different reasons. In her 2024 memoir, Melania: A Memoir, the First Lady discussed her anxieties for Barron regarding media attention, reports the Irish Star.

View 3 Images Barron Trump and Melania Trump (Image: Getty)

‌



She stated: "More than anything, I was concerned for my son, Barron. He was just a young boy, barely ten years old. I dreaded the prospect of media scrutiny toward him.

"Our first year in politics only solidified my determination to shield my son from such unwanted attention. I anticipated that if Donald were to win, the media's scrutiny, deceit, and mistreatment would only escalate."

Nearly a decade after Trump's initial triumph in 2016, Barron has not only matured but reportedly become more engaged with the political aspect of his family's life.

‌



Barron was acknowledged for assisting his father in winning the 2024 US election, with a report suggesting he played a crucial role in advising his father on which podcasts to feature on to secure male voters.

View 3 Images Barron Trump and Melania Trump (Image: Getty)

However, as America's conflict with Iran escalates and his father faces mounting pressure to stabilise global markets as his war drives oil prices ever higher, speculation is growing that a draft may be implemented.

Article continues below

Yet, reintroducing it is something that has reportedly not been dismissed according to the White House's Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt.

During an appearance on Sunday Morning Futures, Ms Leavitt was questioned about whether the Trump administration was contemplating the draft. In response, she stated: "President Trump wisely does not remove options off of the table.

"I know a lot of politicians like to do that quickly but the president as commander in chief wants to continue to assess the success of this military operation It's not part of the current plan right now but again the president wisely keeps his options on the table."

News.title: Civil case against Gerry Adams over IRA bombings withdrawn
News.content: They also suggested it had been taken as an abuse of legal process, and that the civil action was not about trying to prove liability for the three bomb attacks, but to try to establish a much wider process which "the court is not intended to perform or is equipped to perform".

News.title: Norway's crown princess breaks silence on Epstein links: 'I was manipulated and deceived'
News.content: She was also asked whether she had the motivation to remain in her royal role. She has been in poor health and the recent Epstein revelations have raised doubts among many Norwegians that she still has the capacity to become queen, when Crown Prince Haakon accedes to the throne.

A targeted approach would allow more money to be given to households in need and would cost less, at a time when government spending is stretched.

News.content: "It seems to me the portrait reflects so many facets of the album - strength, power, vulnerability, determination and confusion, amongst many others - that it acts as a key to the whole listening experience. I love it."

News.content: This was Greg's third outing for Comic Relief in 10 years, after previously completing five triathlons in five days for his first and climbing mountains Scafell Pike, Snowdon and Ben Nevis for his second.
